meanings.usage: 指用石头、木头、金属等材料雕刻或塑造的艺术品
指在展览中展示的物品
指使物体具有磁性或被磁铁吸引
指将某物拉向自己，与repel（排斥）相对
指推开或抵抗某物，与attract相对
指灯光或火焰不稳定地闪烁
指人类历史记录之前的时期或非常古老的东西
指从上方挂起，通常用绳索或链条
指突然而强烈的短时风
指与众不同的、奇怪的或特有的
指与电子或电子设备相关的
指电流通过身体时造成的感觉或伤害
指基于错误判断的或不正确的

phrases.usage: 表示对某事物不感兴趣，毫不关心

examples.en: Even people who take no interest in art cannot have failed to notice examples of modern sculpture.
examples.zh: 即使对艺术不感兴趣的人也不可能没注意到公共场所的现代雕塑。

examples.en: The objects move in response to a gust of wind.
We have got quite used to them.
examples.zh: 我们已经相当习惯它们了。

examples.en: Some so-called 'modern' pieces have been on display for nearly eighty years.
examples.zh: 一些所谓的'现代'作品已经展出将近八十年了。

examples.en: Lined up against the wall, there were long thin wires attached to metal spheres.
examples.zh: 靠墙排列着系着金属球的细长电线。

examples.en: In the centre of the hall, there were a number of tall structures.
examples.zh: 大厅中央有一些高高的结构物。

examples.en: It was rather like an exhibition of prehistoric electronic equipment.
examples.zh: 它非常像一场史前电子设备的展览。

examples.en: In spite of this, some people were surprised by a recent exhibition.
examples.zh: 尽管如此，一些人仍然对最近的展览感到惊讶。

examples.en: These peculiar forms not only seemed designed to shock people emotionally.
examples.zh: 这些奇特的形状似乎不仅是为了在情感上震撼人们。

phrases.usage: 不可能没注意到，表示一定会注意到

examples.en: Even people who take no interest in art cannot have failed to notice examples of modern sculpture.
examples.zh: 即使对艺术不感兴趣的人也一定会注意到现代雕塑。

grammar.title: 现在完成时 (Present Perfect Tense)
grammar.definition: 表示过去发生的动作或状态持续到现在，或对现在有影响。常与already, yet, just, ever, never, for, since等时间状语连用。
grammar.structure: 主语 + have/has + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于描述从过去开始持续到现在的情况，或强调过去的经验对现在的影响。

examples.en: We have got quite used to them.
examples.zh: 我们已经相当习惯它们了。

examples.en: Some so-called 'modern' pieces have been on display for nearly eighty years.
examples.zh: 一些所谓的'现代'作品已经展出将近八十年了。

examples.en: Even people who take no interest in art cannot have failed to notice examples of modern sculpture.
examples.zh: 即使对艺术不感兴趣的人也一定会注意到现代雕塑。

grammar.title: 被动语态 (Passive Voice)
grammar.definition: 当句子的主语是动作的承受者时使用被动语态。强调动作的接受者或当动作执行者不重要时使用。
grammar.structure: 主语 + be + 过去分词 (+ by + 动作执行者)
grammar.usage: 用于强调动作的承受者，或当动作执行者未知、不重要时。

examples.en: The spheres had been magnetized and attracted or repelled each other.
examples.zh: 这些球已被磁化，彼此相互吸引或排斥。

examples.en: Some so-called 'modern' pieces have been on display for nearly eighty years.
examples.zh: 一些所谓的'现代'作品已经被展出将近八十年了。

examples.en: Sparks were emitted from small black boxes.
examples.zh: 火花从小黑盒子里发射出来。

grammar.title: 情态动词 + have + 过去分词 (Modal + Perfect Infinitive)
grammar.definition: 表示对过去发生的事情的推测、评论或可能性。must have done表示肯定推测，can't have done表示否定推测。
grammar.structure: 情态动词 + have + 过去分词
grammar.usage: 用于表达对过去事件的推测或判断。

examples.en: Even people who take no interest in art cannot have failed to notice examples of modern sculpture.
examples.zh: 即使对艺术不感兴趣的人，也不可能没注意到现代雕塑的例子。

original.en: The idea that modern art can only be seen in museums is mistaken.
original.zh: 认为现代艺术只能在博物馆里看到的观点是错误的。

imitations.en: The idea that money can buy happiness is mistaken.
imitations.zh: 认为金钱能买来幸福的观点是错误的。

imitations.en: The idea that practice makes perfect is mistaken.
imitations.zh: 认为熟能生巧的观点并非总是正确的。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Some + 名词 + have been + 过去分词 + for + 时间段

original.en: Some so-called 'modern' pieces have been on display for nearly eighty years.
original.zh: 一些所谓的'现代'作品已经展出将近八十年了。

imitations.en: These paintings have been in the collection for over a century.
imitations.zh: 这些画作已经被收藏了一个多世纪。

imitations.en: The museum has been open to the public for fifty years.
imitations.zh: 这家博物馆已经向公众开放五十年了。

sentencePatterns.pattern: These + 名词 + were designed to + 动词原形

original.en: These peculiar forms not only seemed designed to shock people emotionally, but to give them electric shocks as well!
original.zh: 这些奇特的形状似乎不仅是为了在情感上震撼人们，而且也是为了给他们电击！

imitations.en: These exercises are designed to improve your speaking skills.
imitations.zh: 这些练习旨在提高你的口语能力。

imitations.en: The warning signs were designed to protect visitors from danger.
imitations.zh: 这些警告标志是为了保护游客免受危险。

sentencePatterns.pattern: Even + 主语 + cannot have failed to + 动词原形

original.en: Even people who take no interest in art cannot have failed to notice examples of modern sculpture on display in public places.
original.zh: 即使对艺术不感兴趣的人，也不可能没注意到公共场所展出的现代雕塑。

imitations.en: Even students who hate history cannot have failed to notice this famous painting.
imitations.zh: 即使讨厌历史的学生也不可能没注意到这幅名画。

imitations.en: Even tourists who speak no Chinese cannot have failed to see the Great Wall.
imitations.zh: 即使不会说中文的游客也一定能看到长城。
